rows.scenario: An author writes one TMR with every field a valid enum member, the skill emits it into tmr-registry.json, and fizzy's validator checks it; the author then flips one field to an unknown enum member and re-submits.
rows.oracle: Jason passes this row iff the valid TMR round-trips skill-emit then fizzy-validate with no field dropped and the unknown-enum TMR produces a named rejection citing the offending field demonstrates the single-shared-schema intent from US-1.
rows.evidence_rationale: the round-tripped record and the named rejection message are concrete artifacts an operator can inspect

rows.scenario: An author writes a user story, authors its happy-path spine TC-0 with named steps, then adds two failure tests — one citing a spine_step_ref and one with none — and runs the authoring lint.
rows.oracle: Jason passes this row iff the lint reports exactly one happy-path spine for the story and rejects the unanchored failure test with a readable 'branch off an unplanted trunk' message demonstrates the one-spine-per-story intent from US-2.
rows.evidence_rationale: authoring is a CLI lint workflow; a transcript shows the authored fixture and the lint's per-finding output

rows.scenario: An author marks a critical-seam test MOCK while naming an inducible technique (tc-netem:partition), then a second author marks a MOCK with live_or_induced null citing a genuine technical_constraint, and both pass through the MOCK-falsification guardrail.
rows.oracle: Jason passes this row iff the technique-naming MOCK is surfaced for promotion to REAL-DATA while the constraint-citing MOCK is accepted demonstrates the no-convenience-mock intent from US-3.
rows.evidence_rationale: the guardrail's promote-vs-accept decision per fixture is visible in the dispatch transcript

rows.scenario: An author has one nl test whose elements all resolve to named concept-accessors and a second nl test whose element maps to no named accessor across rounds; the promoter runs over both.
rows.oracle: Jason passes this row iff the all-accessors-named test advances from nl to acceptance while the undesigned-concept test stays nl and is flagged BLOCK demonstrates the phase-appropriate-rigor intent from US-4.
rows.evidence_rationale: the promoter's per-test promote/block emission is observable in the run transcript

rows.scenario: A conductor submits one spec revision; the orchestrator dispatches the five guardrails as separate subagents and one of them is killed mid-round.
rows.oracle: Jason passes this row iff he sees five separate structured guardrail result sets each keyed to test/US ids and the killed subagent surfaces a blocking ORCH finding rather than a clean green round demonstrates the no-false-confidence intent from US-5.
rows.evidence_rationale: the five separate result sets and the synthetic ORCH are visible in the aggregation transcript

rows.scenario: A spec covers a user journey in prose but has no happy-path spine test for it, and separately omits a minor edge-case test for a journey that does have a spine; TRACE runs over both.
rows.oracle: Jason passes this row iff TRACE reports the spine-less journey as ORPHANED and leaves the missing minor edge test unflagged demonstrates the traceability-break intent from US-6.
rows.evidence_rationale: TRACE's ORPHANED finding and its non-flag on the edge case appear in the guardrail transcript

rows.scenario: TCOV runs over a mock-only critical-seam happy-path TMR and, separately, a mock-only non-critical happy-path TMR.
rows.oracle: Jason passes this row iff TCOV surfaces a blocking missing_liveness_test naming the critical-seam story and leaves the non-critical mock-only path unflagged demonstrates the liveness-hole-detection intent from US-7.
rows.evidence_rationale: the blocking finding (with the named story) versus the clean non-critical case is observable in the TCOV transcript

rows.scenario: An agent attempts a gauntlet-entry transition for a session whose registry has one spine per story, then attempts the same transition for a session missing a spine for one story.
rows.oracle: Jason passes this row iff the full-coverage attempt is admitted and the missing-spine attempt is refused with exit 2 naming the uncovered user story demonstrates the no-unguarded-gauntlet intent from US-8.
rows.evidence_rationale: the admit decision and the exit-2-with-named-story refusal are concrete CLI observables in the transcript

rows.scenario: An operator changes one test's data_strategy from MOCK to REAL-DATA, then changes it again, then replays the per-story journey from the provenance journal.
rows.oracle: Jason passes this row iff the replay shows both changes as ordered driver-tagged records with the first record's bytes unchanged after the second demonstrates the durable-provenance intent from US-9.
rows.evidence_rationale: the ordered journal records and the unchanged prior record are concrete file artifacts

rows.scenario: One node is closed with altitude_fit right and another stable node is closed with altitude_fit too_low; the operator runs the altitude calibration query.
rows.oracle: Jason passes this row iff the calibration output counts the right-fit node as correct and excludes the stable-but-too_low node from the correct-count demonstrates the honest-altitude-calibration intent from US-10.
rows.evidence_rationale: the calibration query output (histogram / precision) is a concrete artifact showing the exclusion

rows.scenario: At Phase-8 close one critical-seam REAL-DATA test is declared but never run, and a second is promoted, run by the skill-runner, and its receipt captured; the close evaluator runs over both.
rows.oracle: Jason passes this row iff the never-run test halts the close as failing while the promoted test with a skill-captured run receipt closes demonstrates the run-it-live-before-close intent from US-11.
rows.evidence_rationale: the halt and the captured run_evidence receipt are observable in the close-pass transcript

rows.scenario: The same missing-spine input is presented to a post-fence session (card created_at after the cutover) and to a legacy session (card created_at before the cutover).
rows.oracle: Jason passes this row iff the post-fence session is refused with exit 2 while the legacy session is left unaffected demonstrates the no-retroactive-failure intent from US-12.
rows.evidence_rationale: the divergent outcomes on the two timestamps are concrete CLI observables in the transcript

rows.scenario: An operator reads the committed CONTEXT.md, document-types reference, and the new ADR after the docs task lands.
rows.oracle: Jason passes this row iff he finds happy-path spine, TMR, maturity ladder and liveness defined with 'happy-path spine' always qualified in prose and an ADR recording the two-spec scope split demonstrates the shared-vocabulary intent from US-13.
rows.evidence_rationale: the committed docs and ADR are concrete artifacts an operator reads directly

rows.scenario: An operator reads the execution plan's task records and the verification-tier lint output.
rows.oracle: Jason passes this row iff every task record carries a verification tier with a consistent canonical verification_mode and no golden-eval member appears demonstrates the tier-driven-verification intent from US-14.
rows.evidence_rationale: the per-task tier/mode declarations in the plan are concrete inspectable artifacts

rows.scenario: A new operator, reading only the Getting Started doc, authors one spine per story on a fixture feature, runs the authoring lint, and runs the F′ checker against a full-coverage fixture and a missing-spine fixture.
rows.oracle: Jason passes this row iff, following only the Getting Started doc, the operator observes the F′ checker print exit 0 on the full-coverage fixture and exit 2 naming the uncovered story on the missing-spine fixture demonstrates the minutes-to-value bootstrap intent from US-15.
rows.evidence_rationale: the documented commands and their exit-code observables are captured end-to-end in a transcript
